name: [BUG-DB-004] TC-DB-004 — Wrong date format DD-MM-YYYY causes HTTP 500 not 400
description:
**[BUG-DB-004]**

Passing dates in DD-MM-YYYY format (common European format) instead of the required YYYY-MM-DD format causes HTTP 500 instead of 400 with format guidance.

**Expected:** HTTP 400 with `"message":"from_date must be in YYYY-MM-DD format"`

**Root Cause:** No format validation — the malformed date string is passed directly to the date parser which throws an unhandled exception.

**Impact:** Developers who pass dates in DD-MM-YYYY (which is technically a valid date string like 01-06-2026) get a confusing 500 with no format guidance.

Root Cause: No format validation — the malformed date string is passed directly to the date parser which throws an unhandled exception.

Impact: Developers who pass dates in DD-MM-YYYY (which is technically a valid date string like 01-06-2026) get a confusing 500 with no format guidance.
